title: The Fine-Tuning Milestone
concept: PHASE D8 COMPLETE. We have mastered low-rank adaptation, NF4 quantization, domain tuning, catastrophic forgetting, and model merging. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D8: Fine-Tuning & Adaptation is complete. Next up: LLM Architectures & Variants.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of fine-tuning an AI model?

options.text: To build a model from absolute scratch
To adapt a pre-trained model to a specific task
To delete all existing knowledge from a model

exercises.explain: Fine-tuning is used to adapt a pre-trained model to specific tasks or domains, rather than starting from scratch.

cards.title: The Fine-Tuning Milestone
cards.body: We have reached the end of Phase D8! You've learned how to take massive, general-purpose models and custom-tailor them for specific tasks using cutting-edge adaptation techniques. Let's review our journey!

cards.title: What is Fine-Tuning Adaptation?
cards.body: Fine-tuning adaptation is the art of taking a large pre-trained model and gently updating its weights to excel at specific tasks, using methods like low-rank adaptation (LoRA), quantization, and model merging to keep it lightweight and smart.

cards.title: The Pastry Masterclass
cards.body: Imagine your AI model is a classically trained chef who knows general cooking. Instead of teaching them to cook all over again, fine-tuning is like giving them a quick masterclass in baking French pastries. You don't replace their knife skills; you just adapt them to a delicious new specialty!

cards.title: Fine-Tuning in Action
cards.body: A medical startup takes a general language model and applies LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) using medical journals. The resulting model can now diagnose symptoms accurately without forgetting basic conversational English.

cards.title: The Specialization Trap
cards.body: Myth: Fine-tuning a model always makes it better at everything. Reality: If you tune too aggressively on one task, the model might experience 'catastrophic forgetting' and lose its general reasoning abilities. Balance is key!

cards.title: Adapting Wisely
cards.body: Compare how a model changes when adapting to new domains.

left.text: Forgetting old skills due to over-tuning on a single new dataset.

right.text: Blending specialized skills while keeping the core knowledge safe.

exercises.prompt: How does Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) make fine-tuning more efficient?

options.text: It trains a small set of adapter weights while keeping original weights frozen.
It deletes half of the model's layers to save memory space.
It completely retraining the model from scratch on new data.
It converts the model into an image classifier.

exercises.explain: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) freezes the original model weights and trains a small set of auxiliary adapter weights, saving massive amounts of compute.

exercises.prompt: Quantization (like NF4) helps run large models on smaller devices by compressing the model weights. True or False?
exercises.explain: Quantization reduces the numerical precision of weights (e.g., to NF4), which drastically lowers memory requirements and lets models run on smaller hardware.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what happens when a model loses its old skills during tuning.
exercises.sentence: Tuning a model too aggressively on new data can cause catastrophic [blank].
exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting occurs when a model is tuned too heavily on new data, causing it to overwrite its previously learned general knowledge.

exercises.prompt: Which chatbot shows the power of model merging (combining two specialized skills)?

options.text: Chatbot A: 'I can write Python code and compose creative poetry at the same time!'
Chatbot B: 'I only know facts up to 2021 and cannot learn new skills.'

exercises.explain: Chatbot A is using a merged model (combining coding and writing adapters), while Chatbot B is a generic model. Chatbot C is just static text.

exercises.prompt: Now that we have finished Phase D8, how do we successfully adapt models using fine-tuning, quantization, and merging without losing their original abilities?

options.text: By deleting the old model and starting training completely from scratch.
By combining specific specialized weights and avoiding overwriting general knowledge.
By only using prompt engineering without altering model parameters.
By forcing the model to forget all its original training data permanently.

exercises.explain: Phase D8: Fine-Tuning & Adaptation is complete. Next up: LLM Architectures & Variants.
